votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the extraction logic and faithfully translates the specification, checking length equality, element preservation at non-multiple-of-3 indices, permutation of extracted elements, and sortedness.
The Python code correctly implements the extraction of every third element and accurately checks the length, unchanged elements, permutation, and sortedness conditions as specified in the Coq code.
The Python code correctly implements the extraction logic and accurately translates the Coq specification, checking length, element equality at non-multiples of 3, permutation, and sortedness.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it checks length equality, verifies non-third positions are unchanged, checks that extracted thirds are permutations of each other (via sorted comparison), and verifies the result's thirds are sorted. The extract_thirds function correctly extracts elements at indices 0, 3, 6, etc. (where i+j % 3 == 0 starting with i=0).

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: extract_thirds extracts elements at indices where (i+j) mod 3 == 0, matching the Coq recursion. sort_third_spec checks: (1) same length, (2) non-third positions unchanged, (3) permutation of thirds via sorted comparison, (4) thirds in result are sorted. The permutation check using sorted(ext_res) == sorted(ext_l) correctly verifies that ext_res is a permutation of ext_l for concrete lists.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: extract_thirds collects elements at indices where (i+j) mod 3 == 0, matching the Coq recursion. sort_third_spec checks: (1) same length, (2) non-third positions unchanged, (3) permutation of thirds via sorted comparison, (4) thirds in res are sorted. The permutation check using sorted(ext_res) == sorted(ext_l) correctly verifies multiset equality, and ext_res == sorted(ext_res) checks sortedness. Both produce the same concrete judgments.

Python extract_thirds matches Coq’s selection of indices i+j ≡ 0 mod 3; sort_third_spec enforces same length, equality at non-multiples of 3, multiset equality of selected thirds (via sorted comparison), and nondecreasing order of those thirds. All concrete behaviors align with the Coq spec.

Python extract_thirds matches Coq’s selection of indices i mod 3 = 0, and sort_third_spec enforces equal length, identical non-multiple-of-3 positions, multiset equality of every-third elements (via sorted equality), and nondecreasing order of those elements—matching the Coq spec on all concrete inputs.
Checks align: equal lengths, positions not multiple of 3 unchanged, extracted multiples-of-3 subsequences are multiset-equal and nondecreasing; extract_thirds behavior matches.
